text: Would you like to join us for lunch?
Why don't we visit the museum together?
That sounds great, but I'm afraid I can't make it.
Hi Pricila! A few of us are going to the old harbor tomorrow. Would you like to join us?
I'd love to! That sounds wonderful. What time are you planning to meet up?
How about meeting at the hotel lobby at ten? Does that work for you?
Could we reschedule for half past ten? I'd like a slow breakfast first.
Of course, half past ten works for everyone. Why don't we have lunch by the water after the walk?
Sounds good -- count me in! I can't wait. See you tomorrow!

Hi Pricila, it's Mark from the tour group. A few of us are getting together tomorrow, and I wanted to invite you. We are planning to meet at the hotel lobby around ten in the morning. The idea is to walk to the old harbor, have lunch by the water, and then visit the little museum nearby. Nothing is fixed yet, so if you would rather start later, just let me know. Are you free tomorrow? It would be lovely to have you with us. Give me a call when you can. Talk soon.

When I first started traveling, I always waited for other people to invite me. I was too shy to make plans myself. But on this trip, something changed. Last week, I was the one who said, why don't we all have dinner together? To my surprise, everyone said yes. Now I understand that an invitation is just a kind way to open a door. You suggest something simple, you stay flexible, and you let the other person choose. Making plans in English is not about perfect grammar. It is about being warm and saying, come and join us.

First, the traveler invites Pricila to spend the day together. Then he suggests a time and a place to meet. Next, Pricila asks if they can start a little later. After that, they agree on a new time that works for both. Finally, Pricila happily accepts and says she cannot wait.
Would you like to join us for dinner?
How about meeting at the lobby at ten?
Why don't we walk to the harbor?
That sounds good, count me in!
I'm afraid I can't make it today.
Would you like to join us for lunch?
